commit.message:
fix: use COALESCE(userId, visitorId) for reliable unique visitor counting

Logged-in users are now identified by userId (stable across devices/browsers)
instead of visitorId (localStorage UUID, easily reset). Anonymous visitors
still use visitorId as fallback. Applied to all 5 unique count queries:
topEvents, topRaces, viewsPerDay, totalUniqueVisitors, newVisitorsPerDay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add supersets (#3) (#9)

* feat: add supersets — group exercises into merged cards with shared rounds

- DB migration v7: new superset_groups table, superset_group_id and superset_position columns on workout_exercises
- New SupersetGroup model and type; WorkoutExercise/WorkoutFull extended with superset fields
- Service layer: makeSuperset, addExerciseToSuperset, disbandSuperset, updateSupersetRestSeconds, logSupersetRound; repeatWorkout copies superset structure; removeExerciseFromWorkout auto-disbands when ≤1 exercise remains
- New SupersetCard component: merged card with per-round logging, shared rest timer, Add Exercise and Ungroup actions
- ExerciseCard: Make Superset badge button
- Active workout screen: groups exercises into render items, unified ExercisePicker mode, superset-aware reordering

Closes #3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: superset — pre-create sets for added exercises, show last time inline on label

- addExerciseToSuperset now creates empty sets matching existing round count so input fields appear immediately for newly added exercises
- SupersetCard shows last session data inline on the exercise label row (e.g. "Bench Press  last: 80kg × 10") instead of below each set row, making rounds more compact

When an exercise in a superset is missing a set for a round (e.g. after

SetRow that creates the set with data on save, instead of an inert

via a precomputed renderItems array. Exercise name column uses natural width
so values always align next to labels regardless of name length.

  Kruti Dev-encoded label detection (ysf[kdk% = लेखिका:, izdk'kd% = प्रकाशक:)

Fix menu drawer width: override theme CSS at source in snippet

The snippet's own {% stylesheet %} block was overriding base.css
drawer positioning with left:0 and width:25rem on desktop.
Added RRUGA overrides directly in header-drawer.liquid stylesheet
to match 40% pill nav: left:50%, width:calc(40vw-20px), centered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace hardcoded colors with hh-* design tokens across 44 components

Migrates ~1000+ violations from hardcoded Tailwind colors and inline hex
values to the hh-* design token system. Adds admin-session class to
AdminLayout root wrapper so hh-primary resolves to purple in admin context.

Key mappings: purple→hh-primary, emerald/green→hh-success, red→hh-error,
amber/orange→hh-warning, gray/slate→hh-muted/hh-ui-*, inline rgba→color-mix.

refactor: replace ImperativeParser dirty-flags with ReactiveParser Signal/Memo pipeline (#16)

* chore(editor): add dowdiness/loom dependency for ReactiveParser

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(editor): add ReactiveParser idempotency invariant test

* refactor(editor): replace ImperativeParser struct with ReactiveParser

* refactor(editor): replace dirty-flag mutations with set_source calls

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(editor): replace ImperativeParser dirty-flags with ReactiveParser Signal/Memo

Remove parse_dirty, cached_text, cached_errors, ast fields and reparse().
ReactiveParser.set_source() is called after each mutation; term() and
diagnostics() are memoized — no manual cache invalidation needed.

Add dowdiness/seam as a direct dependency in moon.mod.json and editor/moon.pkg
so the Eq impl for SyntaxNode (required by ReactiveParser::term) is visible
at the editor package's call sites.

Implements Design §2 Strategy A.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(editor): switch get_ast() to return Term via syntax_node_to_term

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(editor): update parsed_editor tests to use print_term (Term API)

Replace all @ast.print_ast_node() calls with @ast.print_term() in
parsed_editor_test.mbt, and rewrite test_error_recovery.mbt to use
editor.get_errors() instead of the removed @parser.collect_errors(ast)
API. All 73 editor tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: switch get_ast_dot to term_to_dot after AstNode removal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(projection): migrate from AstNode/AstKind to Term

Replace all AstNode/AstKind usage with Term pattern matching.
Define local ProjNode type to preserve node IDs and positions.
Leaf nodes are Int/Var/Error; labels derived directly from Term variants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: advance loom submodule and complete AstNode→Term migration

- Loom advanced to latest (Term::Error variant; AstNode removed)
- Term gains ToJson for JSON bridge
- ParsedEditor.get_ast() returns Term via syntax_node_to_term
- projection/ defines local ProjNode (ID+position-aware wrapper for Term)
- All tests pass (182 tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify and clean up reactive pipeline changes

- Delegate is_parse_valid to get_errors() for consistency
- Fix proj_node_has_errors to early-return on first error found
- Remove duplicate unregister_node_tree from text_lens.mbt
- Move same_kind_tag to proj_node.mbt where it logically belongs
- Document Error as leaf node in is_leaf_node

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update loom submodule pointer after rebase onto origin/main

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(projection): handle Term::Unit variant added in loom rebase

New loom commit bbaf282 added Unit to the Term enum for definition-only
source files. Add Unit cases to get_node_label, placeholder_text match
in InsertChild, and same_kind_tag to fix partial_match compile errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(projection): make same_kind_tag package-private after move to proj_node

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(projection): rebuild ProjNode.kind from reconciled children to keep kind/children in sync

ProjNode.kind embeds the full @ast.Term subtree. Text rendering via
print_term(ast.kind) reads kind recursively — it never reads .children.
After reconciliation or tree mutations, .children was updated but .kind
kept stale embedded sub-terms, so same-shape edits (x+1 → x+2) were
silently lost in text rendering.

Fix: add rebuild_kind(shape, children) to reconstruct the embedded term
from reconciled ProjNode children. Apply it in:
- reconcile_ast: all composite branches (Lam, App, Bop, If, Let)
- update_node_in_tree: ancestor nodes rebuilt after child replacement
- remove_child_at / insert_child_at: same structural consistency fix

Add regression test: text_lens_put should update rendered text after
same-shape edit (x+1 → x+2 must render as x+2, not x+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(projection): make term_to_proj_node private; fix test name

term_to_proj_node is only called within proj_node.mbt (recursively and
from parse_to_proj_node). Remove pub to keep it out of the public API;
moon info removes it from pkg.generated.mbti automatically.

Rename test to accurately reflect what it verifies: that two consecutive
get_ast() calls return identical output, not that parsing was skipped.

Skipped: position ranges in term_to_proj_node — @ast.Term carries no
span data; proper per-node ranges require propagating SyntaxNode spans
before Term conversion (known limitation, documented in comment).
Skipped: migration note in .mbti — generated file, edits overwritten by
moon info; project has no existing migration guide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(projection): replace term_to_proj_node with syntax_to_proj_node for accurate spans

Walk @seam.SyntaxNode directly instead of @ast.Term to preserve
per-node start/end positions from the CST. Each ProjNode now gets
the actual source span of the corresponding syntax node rather than
the full text range [0, text.length()].

Add syntax_to_proj_node with typed CST views (IntLiteralView,
VarRefView, LambdaExprView, AppExprView, BinaryExprView, IfExprView,
LetExprView, ParenExprView) for accurate span extraction.
Add dowdiness/seam dependency to projection/moon.pkg.

Tests: parse_to_proj_node preserves child-specific ranges,
node_at_position distinguishes left and right operands.

commit.message:
Add brownfield awareness: safe installation on existing codebases

Orchestrator now detects greenfield vs brownfield at startup by
scanning for existing source files, frameworks, and infrastructure.

Brownfield mode:
- Auto-generates .production-grade.yaml from discovered structure
- Writes codebase-context.md with rules for all agents
- User approves path mapping before any BUILD phase

All BUILD/SHIP skills (software-engineer, frontend-engineer, devops,
qa-engineer, solution-architect, sre, technical-writer) now load
codebase context and follow brownfield rules: read existing code
first, match existing style, never overwrite, extend don't replace.

commit.message:
feat: add thresholdPercent for percentage-based diff tolerance

Adds a new `thresholdPercent` config option (default: 0.5%) alongside the
existing absolute pixel `threshold`. A test passes when either threshold is
satisfied, making it resilient to font antialiasing and subpixel rendering
differences across environments.

Also includes: TUI error/warning sublines, keyboard scrolling (j/k,
Ctrl+U/D, PageUp/PageDown), keyboard thread fix, summary bar shortcuts,
dimension mismatch handling (width warns, height silent), and directory
cleanup before test runs.

that previously only existed on the Windows machine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(screenshot): report onFailure error immediately to backend

Without this, when takeScreenshot() fails (e.g. emulator limitation),
onFailure was logged but no result was POSTed back to the backend.
The backend would then hang for 10s and Railway's proxy would return
a raw 504 before our JSON timeout response.

Fix: postScreenshotResult(null, errorMsg) in onFailure and null-bitmap paths.
Backend screenshot-result now rejects the promise on error field, returning
500 with the device error message instead of a proxy-level 504.

audit kmp roadmap AjuNk (#116)

* fix(backend): add missing install import to all 3 Application.kt files

All three backend services (api, license, sync) failed Docker image
builds with 'Unresolved reference: install' because
io.ktor.server.application.install was not explicitly imported.
In Ktor 3.x the install extension function must be imported separately
from the Application class itself.

Fixes build-backend-images CI failure (SHA 0c29487 / run 22708434211).

https://claude.ai/code/session_0195xibztDGTtaHvqNKVstKz

* fix(sync): use pingPeriodMillis/timeoutMillis in WebSockets config (Ktor 3.x)

Ktor 3.x renamed the WebSocket plugin configuration properties:
- pingPeriod  → pingPeriodMillis (Long, milliseconds)
- timeout     → timeoutMillis    (Long, milliseconds)

The old Duration-based names don't exist in WebSocketOptions for
Ktor 3.4.1, causing 'Unresolved reference' compile errors in the
build-backend-images (sync) CI job.

Removes the unused kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.seconds import.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0195xibztDGTtaHvqNKVstKz

* ci(vps): add one-time VPS setup workflow

Bootstraps /opt/zyntapos on the Contabo VPS via SSH using existing
secrets (VPS_HOST, VPS_USER, DEPLOY_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, PAT_TOKEN).

Steps: installs Docker, clones repo, generates REDIS_PASSWORD,
pulls GHCR images, starts the stack.

Trigger: Actions → "VPS First-Time Setup" → Run workflow → type "setup"
Delete this workflow after first successful run.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0195xibztDGTtaHvqNKVstKz
